info.name: Font
info.comment: Draws bitmap fonts and manages font meta information.

description.p: The Font class renders fixed-size bitmap fonts to a Bitmap and exposes metrics for the selected font face. It supports bold, italic and underlined text, as well as adjustable glyph spacing, line spacing, tab width and alignment. Bitmap fonts are loaded from Windows .fon files. Scalable TrueType rendering is provided by VectorText instead.

Bitmap fonts must be stored in the fonts:fixed/ directory to be recognised during font database refreshes. Use the Font module's query and refresh functions to inspect or rebuild the installed font list.

Font strings are interpreted as UTF-8, allowing Unicode text to be supplied through the String field. Do not insert arbitrary byte values above 127 unless they are valid UTF-8 sequences. Invalid or unsupported glyphs fall back to the font's default character.

Initialisation of a new Font object can be as simple as declaring its Point size and Face name. Font face, size and style selections should be set before initialisation because the loaded bitmap data is cached for that combination. To use multiple styles of the same face, create one Font object for each required style. Runtime drawing properties such as colour, String, X, Y and alignment can still be changed between draw operations.

To draw a string, set Bitmap and String, then call Draw. The X and Y fields set the starting position. Use Align together with AlignWidth and AlignHeight when text needs to be positioned within a larger surface area.
This documentation uses the following font terminology:

list.li: Point is the requested size of the font. It is relative to other point sizes of the same face; two faces at the same point size are not necessarily the same pixel height.
Height is the vertical bearing of the font expressed in pixels. It excludes top leading and the gutter used by descending glyphs.
Gutter is the space below the baseline used by descending glyphs such as g and y. It is also known as the external leading or descent.
LineSpacing is the recommended pixel distance from one baseline to the next.
Glyph is a single rendered character image.

description.p: Use VectorText for most display text. The Font class draws directly to a Bitmap and is not integrated with the display vector scene graph.

action.name: Draw
action.comment: Draws a font to a Bitmap.
action.prototype: ERR acDraw(*Object, DOUBLE X, DOUBLE Y, DOUBLE Width, DOUBLE Height)

input.param: The X position of the region to be drawn.
The Y position of the region to be drawn.
The width of the region to be drawn.
The height of the region to be drawn.

description.p: Draws String to the target Bitmap, starting at X and Y after any configured alignment and baseline adjustments have been applied.

result.error: The Bitmap field has not been set.

field.name: Align
field.comment: Sets the position of a font string to an abstract alignment.

description.p: Sets the alignment of the font string within the AlignWidth and AlignHeight area. This is used in addition to the X and Y drawing coordinates.

field.name: AlignHeight
field.comment: The height to use when aligning the font string.

description.p: Defines the height of the area used for vertical alignment. If this field is 0, the target Bitmap height is used.

field.name: AlignWidth
field.comment: The width to use when aligning the font string.

description.p: Defines the width of the area used for horizontal alignment. If this field is 0, the target Bitmap width is used.

field.name: Ascent
field.comment: The total number of pixels above the baseline.

description.p: Reflects the total number of pixels above the baseline, including Leading.

field.name: Bitmap
field.comment: The destination Bitmap to use when drawing a font.

field.name: Bold
field.comment: Set to true to enable bold styling.

description.p: Setting this field before initialisation selects the Bold style, or Bold Italic if Italic is also set. Prefer Style when setting an exact style name.

field.name: Colour
field.comment: The font colour in RGB8 format.

field.name: EndX
field.comment: Indicates the final horizontal coordinate after completing a draw operation.

description.p: Reflects the final horizontal coordinate reached by the most recent Draw operation.

field.name: EndY
field.comment: Indicates the final vertical coordinate after completing a draw operation.

description.p: Reflects the final vertical coordinate reached by the most recent Draw operation.

field.name: Face
field.comment: The name of a font face that is to be loaded on initialisation.

description.p: The name of an installed font face must be specified before initialisation unless Path is set directly. A list of available faces can be obtained from GetList.
The face string can include optional point size, style and colour values in the form face:pointsize:style:colour. Omitted middle values may be left empty.
Here are some examples:
Multiple font faces can be specified in CSV format, e.g. Sans Serif,Noto Sans. Names are resolved from left to right.

field.name: FixedWidth
field.comment: Forces a fixed pixel width to use for all glyphs.

description.p: Forces all glyphs to advance by the specified pixel width. If the value is less than the widest glyph, rendered glyphs can overlap.

field.name: Flags
field.comment: Optional flags.

field.name: GlyphSpacing
field.comment: Adjusts the amount of spacing between each character.

description.p: Adjusts horizontal glyph advance as a multiplier of each glyph's normal width. The default value is 1.0.
Using negative values is valid, and can lead to text being printed backwards.

field.name: Gutter
field.comment: The 'external leading' value, measured in pixels. Applies to fixed fonts only.

description.p: Reflects the external leading, or descent area, below the baseline.

field.name: Height
field.comment: The point size of the font, expressed in pixels.

description.p: Reflects the initialised font height in pixels. It does not include Leading; use Ascent when the leading-inclusive distance above the baseline is required.
The height is calculated on initialisation and can be read at any time.

field.name: Italic
field.comment: Set to true to enable italic styling.

description.p: Setting this field before initialisation selects the Italic style, or Bold Italic if Bold is also set. Prefer Style when setting an exact style name.

field.name: Leading
field.comment: 'Internal leading' measured in pixels. Applies to fixed fonts only.

field.name: LineCount
field.comment: The total number of lines in a font string.

description.p: Returns the number of lines in String. If WrapEdge is set, wrapped lines are included in the count.

field.name: LineSpacing
field.comment: The amount of spacing between each line.

description.p: Defines the vertical distance from one line baseline to the next. It is initialised from the selected font and can be increased or decreased to adjust line spacing. If negative, later lines are drawn upward.

If set before initialisation, the value is added to the font's normal line spacing instead of replacing it. For instance, setting the LineSpacing to 2 will result in an extra 2 pixels being added to the font's spacing.

field.name: MaxHeight
field.comment: The maximum possible pixel height per character.

description.p: Reflects the maximum bitmap height for the selected font at the current point size.

field.name: Opacity
field.comment: Determines the level of translucency applied to a font.

description.p: Determines the translucency level of the font fill colour. The default setting is 100, meaning fully opaque. Lower values blend the glyphs with the destination Bitmap.
Please note that the use of translucency will always have an impact on the time it normally takes to draw a font.

field.name: Outline
field.comment: Defines the outline colour around a font.

description.p: Draws a one-pixel outline around bitmap glyphs when set to an RGB8 colour with a non-zero alpha component. Set the field to NULL or use an alpha value of zero to disable outlining.

field.name: Path
field.comment: The path to a font file.

description.p: Defines the exact font file to load before initialisation. This bypasses normal face-name resolution through the font database.
This feature is ideal for use when distributing custom fonts with an application.

field.name: Point
field.comment: The point size of a font.

description.p: Defines the requested font size in points.

When setting the point size of a bitmap font, the system will try and find the closest matching value for the requested point size. For instance, if you request a fixed font at point 11 and the closest size is point 8, the system will drop the font to point 8.

field.name: String
field.comment: The string to use when drawing a Font.

description.p: The String field must be defined to draw text with a Font object. It must contain valid UTF-8. Line feed characters start a new line during Draw.
If a string contains characters that are not supported by a font, those characters will be printed using a default character from the font.

field.name: Style
field.comment: Determines font styling.

description.p: Selects the preferred style for initialisation. If the selected face does not provide that style, regular styling or the first registered style is used.
Bitmap fonts are a special case if a bold or italic style is selected. In this situation the system can automatically convert the font to that style even if the correct graphics set does not exist.
Conventional font styles are Bold, Bold Italic, Italic and Regular (the default).

field.name: TabSize
field.comment: Defines the tab size to use when drawing and manipulating a font string.

description.p: Controls the tab interval, measured in character columns.
The default tab size is 8. This field only affects tab characters in String.

field.name: Underline
field.comment: Enables font underlining when set.

description.p: Draws an underline using the supplied RGB8 colour. Set the field to NULL or use an alpha value of zero to disable underlining.

field.name: Width
field.comment: Returns the pixel width of a string.

description.p: Returns the pixel width of String using the current font metrics and wrapping settings. If String is empty, the result is 0.

field.name: WrapEdge
field.comment: Enables word wrapping at a given boundary.

description.p: Enables word wrapping when set to a value greater than zero. Wrapping occurs when a word extends beyond this X coordinate.

field.name: X
field.comment: The starting horizontal position when drawing the font string.

description.p: Defines the starting horizontal coordinate for Draw. The default coordinate is 0.

field.name: Y
field.comment: The starting vertical position when drawing the font string.

description.p: Defines the starting vertical coordinate for Draw. The default coordinate is 0.

field.name: YOffset
field.comment: Additional offset value that is added to vertically aligned fonts.

description.p: Returns the vertical offset applied by VERTICAL or BOTTOM alignment. Add this value to Y to determine the aligned drawing baseline.

constants.comment: Universal values for alignment of graphics and text
Result flags for the SelectFont() function.
constants.const: The font should not appear in any named list shown to the user.
The Freetype hinter should be used.
The light version of the Freetype hinter should be used.
The hinting information provided by the font should be given preference.
The font is scalable (assume fixed otherwise).
This is a scalable font featuring variable metrics.
The Font's Y coordinate is the base line.
Font is described as having a bold weight (read only).
Underline the font with a double-sized line, using the colour defined in Underline.
Font is described as using italics (read only).

struct.name: FontList
struct.comment: For GetList(), describes a system font.
struct.field: Pointer to the next entry in the list.
The name of the font face.
Reference to another font Name if this is an alias.
Pointer to an array of fixed point sizes supported by the font.
Supported styles are listed here in CSV format.
For variable fonts, lists all supported axis codes in CSV format
TRUE if the font is scalable.
TRUE if the font has variable metrics.
TRUE if the font should be hidden from user font lists.
